column11:
We hope you have settled into your new role!
   
Our goal is for all new employees to feel welcomed in the Wake County Public School System and get up to speed with their roles as quickly and smoothly as they can. In order for us to improve the onboarding experience for future employees, we'd love your feedback on your experience so far. Thank you in advance for taking the time to give your thoughts. 

All of the feedback you give on this survey will be completely confidential. Your supervisor (i.e., Administrator, Principal) will not have access to your individual feedback, and results will only be reported in aggregate, in groups of 5 responses or more. In some places on the survey, we may ask you for comment feedback. Comments will be reported back as written, so if you are concerned about being identified, we advise you to take care not to disclose information in your comments that could identify you.
This survey should take you less than five minutes and will ask you about the following:
     
  • Your job 
  • Your team 
  • Any training you’ve received 
  • Your supervisor 
  • Your overall experience so far 

We want to get your feedback while it's fresh in your mind, so please take the survey as soon as possible and in one sitting. Simply answer as honestly as you can, and if you don't know the answer to any of the questions, you can leave them blank. Click below to begin your survey.

column5: Survey for WCPSS employees who leave the school system.
column11:
Before you go, one last thing.
 
We need your honest feedback on what it was like to work here.  As a district, we want to provide the best possible working environment for all employees. This exit survey is an opportunity for us to get open and honest feedback from you about your experiences working here, so that we can improve for current and future employees. 

We're really interested to hear your views and want to thank you in advance for taking the time to give your feedback!

A quick note on confidentiality
 
All of the feedback you give on this survey will be completely confidential. Your supervisor will not have access to your individual feedback, and results will only be reported in aggregate, in groups of 5 responses or more.
 
In some places on the survey, we may ask you to share comments. Comments will be reported back as written, so if you are concerned about being identified, do not to disclose information in your comments that could identify you.

Welcome to our Mentor Training Survey

Effective mentor training for teachers goes beyond simply pairing a veteran educator with a newcomer; it focuses on developing the specific coaching and other skill sets required to support another professional’s growth. 

Let us know how you enjoyed the training!

column5: This collection is to test the Staff Only columns.
Students who are ready to earn high school credit by showing what they know, without taking the course, can apply for Credit by Demonstrated Mastery (CDM).
column11:
Welcome to the CDM Application!

Step 1: Submit Your CDM Application
Students interested in earning credit through CDM must submit the CDM Application and Agreement to their school’s CDM coordinator.
Deadline: Typically in September or October.

Step 2: Meet with Your School Counselor
Before testing, students will meet with a counselor to:
Understand how CDM affects their transcript, GPA, and class rank
Review how CDM fits into their four-year graduation plan
Timing: Most counseling takes place November–January, depending on the testing schedule.

Step 3: Take the Phase 1 Assessment
Phase 1 is a multiple-choice exam that measures core knowledge of the course standards.

Testing Window: Final five days of first semester
Results Shared: January–February
If You Pass: You’ll receive instructions to begin Phase 2.
If You Do Not Pass: The CDM process ends.
Important Note: This attempt will not appear on your transcript.

Step 4: Complete the Phase 2 Assessment
Phase 2 measures how well you can apply your knowledge. You may be asked to:

Complete a task aligned to course standards such as an assessment, portfolio, or artifact. 
Submissions Due: By end of March
Results Shared: In April by a district CDM Review Panel
If You Pass: Credit will be added to your transcript with the label CDM—no letter grade is assigned.
If You Do Not Pass: No credit is awarded, and there’s no record on your transcript.

CDM Available Courses 
The courses below are those available for the Credit by Demonstrated Mastery (CDM) process for the 2025-26 school year. Included is the type of assessment and minimum score required for Phase 1.

English I, locally-developed exam, 90% 
English II, EOC, level V Scale Score ≥ 565
NC Math 1, EOC,  level V Scale Score ≥ 563
NC Math 2, locally-developed exam, 90%
NC Math 3, EOC, level V Scale Score ≥ 563
Biology, EOC, level V Scale Score ≥ 361
Spanish, French, German, Proficiency Exam, Novice High
Career and Technical Education CDM Courses
Career and Technical Education (CTE) requires completion of CTE State Assessments with minimum score of 90%. Students may only apply for courses that are offered at their school.  

BB40 Business Management I
BF21 Financial Planning I
GS11 Project Management I
FI21 Interior Design Fundamentals
FN41 Food and Nutrition I
HU40 Health Science I

column5: Please fill out the form below for applying for a grant. Please wait 24-36 hours for us to get back to you. Thank you!
column11:
***PLEASE NOTE:  ONLY PRINCIPALS OR SCHOOL GRANT CONTACTS MAY COMPLETE THIS FORM.***

Principals and School Grant Contacts should log into their WCPSS Google accounts to complete and as partially form. Please be sure to have all the needed information ready as partially completed forms cannot be saved. ***  For a preview, visit https://goo.gl/e8Yr9g
 
To ensure that WCPSS policies are followed, please work with your Principal or School Grant Contact to submit this form for each grant you plan to pursue, answering the questions to the best of your ability. WCPSS Board approval is required for all grant submissions, and the Office of Grants will manage the approval process for you. Please do not submit a grant until you receive notification from the Office of Grants that the funding opportunity is Board approved. Be sure to keep a file of all your grant materials, including this Intent to Apply form, your proposal, budget, and any other relevant materials.

***PLEASE DO NOT SUBMIT THIS FORM FOR ONLINE CLASSROOM WISHLISTS SUCH AS AMAZON, ETC.*** 

Questions? Visit our website for additional information, or email us at grants@wcpss.net.

Per Board Policy 8100 - The budget resolution shall conform to the uniform budget format (GS115C-426) established by the State Board of Education.  WCPSS Budget Resolution adopted October 7, 2014 (page 30, B1-4).

If information changes after the form is submitted, please contact us (grants@wcpss.net) to update the information.

column5: This is a test collection. Select "Edit Form" to view details. You can create as many collections as needed for your organization; there is no limit. Responses can be Authenticated or Anonymous, and all collection forms are mobile friendly! The link to the survey is under the Share tab.
column11:
Welcome!
You can add detailed instructions for your collection as needed here. Add multiple fields and pages under Questions. Attachments are not functional in demo mode.

What is a "Ticket"?
A ticket is a way for internal staff to keep track of notes for each submission in a collection. Just like a form, you can design a ticket to hold the fields you need for your team to keep track of information after the submission. Once you have a Ticket set up you can assign it from the "Add Ticket" section in your collection. 

Adding information for a submission
To add information to a submission, click on Records > Individual > Select a Submission. You can leave a Comment, or if a Ticket is assigned to the collection, you can fill in the fields created for the Ticket. You can also add a "Staff Only" field type. "Staff Only" field types are only viewable by staff and show under the submitted item.

Approvals
If you need someone to approve a submission, you can enable Approvals and assign a Person or Role. Approvals are currently in development.

Seed Data
To add sample data for a collection, go to Settings > Seed Data.

Adding additional staff
Any WCPSS staff member can use this application. If you want to restrict them to certain aspects of the application, give them the Reviewer role. A Reviewer cannot edit a collection. This may be expanded to different types of Roles in the future.

Staff Only Fields
Columns marked as "Staff Only" as only visible by staff, and can be accessed from Records > Individual > View submission.
